name: Cassie's Egg Cornbread
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve the baking soda in the buttermilk; stir well.
2. In a separate bowl, combine the cornmeal, baking powder, and sugar.
3. Add the eggs and buttermilk mixture to the cornmeal mixture, stirring until just combined.
4. Heat a well-greased cast-iron skillet in a 400-degree oven for 3 minutes or until very hot.
5. Pour the batter into the hot skillet.
6. Bake at 425 degrees for 30 minutes or until lightly browned.
Cassie was a cook serving the residents of Tolville, Arkansas from the late 1920s through the 1940s. Her Egg Cornbread was a favorite.
